description: Citation Faithfulness Benchmark: 30 cited-writing prompts across 4 categories. For Study 1 in the ScholarHub evaluation chapter.

categories.introduction: Open a paper with a 1-paragraph context and citations
categories.methodology: Justify a methodological choice with citations
categories.discussion: Place a finding in the literature with citations

prompts.prompt: Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on long-context transformer architectures. Include 3 citations to seminal work.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on diffusion-based image generation. Cite 3 foundational papers.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on graph neural networks for molecular property prediction. Cite 3 prior works.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on reinforcement learning from human feedback. Cite 3 relevant papers.
Write a one-paragraph introduction motivating low-resource neural machine translation. Cite 3 prior works.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on federated learning for healthcare data. Cite 3 prior works.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on neural architecture search. Cite 3 prior works.
Write a one-paragraph introduction to a paper on self-supervised representation learning for vision. Cite 3 prior works.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section synthesizing the literature on retrieval-augmented generation. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on conflict-free replicated data types for collaborative editing. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on operational transformation in groupware systems. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on AI-assisted academic writing tools. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on citation hallucination in large language models. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on vector database systems for semantic search. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section on tool-use architectures for LLM agents. Cite 3 papers.
Write a one-paragraph related-work section comparing CRDTs and operational transformation for real-time collaboration. Cite 3 papers.
Justify the use of BLEU score for evaluating a machine translation system in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify the choice of 5-fold cross-validation for a small medical imaging dataset in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify using GPT-4 as a judge for evaluating LLM outputs in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify the use of Bonferroni correction for multiple hypothesis testing in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify using pgvector for vector similarity search in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify using Y.js for collaborative document editing in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Justify using OpenAI function calling for tool orchestration in a one-paragraph methodology section. Cite 3 papers.
Place this finding in the literature: a retrieval-grounded LLM achieved a 0% citation fabrication rate. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss this finding in context: 95th-percentile concurrent edit latency below 200ms with 10 editors. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss this finding: aggregating five paper sources increased recall by 35% over the best single source. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss this finding: a 30-tool agent covered 92% of researcher requests without further prompt tuning. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss this finding: an integrated workspace reduced application context switches per task by 78%. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss this finding: a CRDT-based editor scaled to 50 concurrent users without throughput degradation. One paragraph, 3 citations.
Discuss the design implications of using PostgreSQL row-level security for multi-tenant isolation. One paragraph, 3 citations.
